extracted_json.title: Step 2: Five (or Six) Forces Analysis — Process

blocks.text: Step 2: Perform a five (or six) forces analysis of the I/M landscape or structure

items.text: For each force, there is a set of determinants that affect the intensity (or strength) of the force
Example: Force 1 (Rivalry between competitors) is affected by concentration (or # of competitors), the size (or Sales Revenue $, of each competitor)
A list of determinants for each of the 5 forces is given in Table 1 of the LNCS handout (column 2)
For each force, determine the qualitative strength of that force (High, Medium, Low) based on the determinants in Table 1, column 2
Table 1, Column 3, shows the qualitative strength of each of the 5 forces for the PC industry in the 1980s

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Step 2 of strategic analysis: performing a five (or six) forces analysis of industry structure.
extracted_json.summary_gist: Outlines the process for a five/six forces analysis of an industry/market landscape. Each force has determinants that affect its intensity (e.g., Force 1 Rivalry is affected by concentration and competitor size/sales revenue). Determinants for the 5 forces are listed in Table 1 column 2 of the LNCS handout; the qualitative strength (High/Medium/Low) of each force is determined from these, with Table 1 column 3 showing strengths for the 1980s PC industry.

transcription:
Step 2 : Perform a five (or six) forces analysis
          of the I/M landscape or structure

Process :
(a) — For each force, there is a set of
       determinants that affect the intensity
       (or strength) of the force

Example : Force 1 ( Rivalry between
          competitors) is affected by
          concentration (or # of competitors),
          the size (or Sales Revenue $, of
          each competitor)

(b) — A list of determinants for each of
      the 5 forces is given in Table 1 of
      the LNCS handout (column 2)

(c) — For each force, determine the
      qualitative strength of that force
      ( High, Medium, Low) based on the
      determinants in Table 1, column 2

(d) — Table 1, Column 3, shows the qualitative
      strength of each of the 5 forces for
      the PC industry in the 1980s
